title: Baccharis glomeruliflora
taxonomy: Baccharis glomeruliflora belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Baccharis glomeruliflora is specifically classified under the subfamily Asteroideae. The genus Baccharis itself is a group of shrubs and trees that are native to the Americas, and Baccharis glomeruliflora is one of the species within this genus. Baccharis glomeruliflora is a specific species of Baccharis that is native to South America. Its classification is also supported by molecular studies that have confirmed its phylogenetic relationships within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Baccharis glomeruliflora is a plant native to South America, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. In addition, Baccharis glomeruliflora has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion. The plant's leaves and stems are also used to make a tea that is believed to have antioxidant and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses of Baccharis glomeruliflora.

constituents:
I can find some information on the active constituents of Baccharis glomeruliflora. 

Baccharis glomeruliflora contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids which are the main active constituents responsible for its medicinal properties. intro: Baccharis glomeruliflora, also known as the cluster false hebe, is a shrub native to the Andean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, Baccharis glomeruliflora is used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis glomeruliflora include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Baccharis glomeruliflora is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and skin conditions.
